label: Other Bank or Credit Union
description: Update your legal name at other financial institutions — banks, credit unions, or investment accounts. The general process is similar across most institutions: in-person visit, updated ID, and name change documentation.

process.summary: Contact your financial institution to confirm their specific name change process, then visit in person with your updated ID and legal name change documentation. Most institutions follow a similar process.

steps.description: Call or check your institution's website to confirm what documents they require for a legal name change. Requirements vary slightly by institution.
Gather your updated government-issued photo ID (driver's license or passport) and your legal name change documentation (court order).
Visit the institution in person. Request a legal name update on all accounts. Ask about debit and credit card replacements at the same time.
steps.note: Some credit unions and smaller banks may also accept mailed documentation — ask when you call ahead.

steps.description: Confirm the update in your online banking profile and review any automatic payments or linked accounts.

access_requirements.travel_note: An in-person visit is typically required. Call ahead to confirm.

process.estimated_time_days: Varies by institution. Typically same-day update with new cards in 5 to 10 business days.
process.notes: If you have investment accounts (brokerage, IRA, 401k), those may have their own name change process — often handled through the institution's customer service line or a separate form.

discrimination_notes: The Equal Credit Opportunity Act and other federal laws protect against discrimination in financial services. The CFPB (consumerfinance.gov) handles complaints about financial institution discrimination.

presence_level_content.some_guidance: If you have accounts at multiple smaller institutions, it can help to call each one first — some will accept mailed documents, which saves in-person trips.
